clusters.title: Philadelphia Schools Now Guarantee Water and Bathroom Breaks and Daily Recess–With a Ban on Silent Lunches
clusters.summary: In a decision that one advocacy group said would “make dignity non-negotiable,” Philadelphia schools have adopted a new wellness program that will guarantee recess and bathroom breaks. The program also prevents teachers from withdrawing access to these through disciplinary measures, and guarantees “movement breaks” for every 90 minutes of seat time in elementary schools. It […] The post Philadelphia Schools Now Guarantee Water and Bathroom Breaks and Daily Recess–With a Ban on Silent Lunches appeared first on Good News Network.

clusters.title: Kazakhstan Plants 37,000 Seedlings to Prepare for Imminent Return of Tigers
clusters.summary: Kazakhstan is preparing to reintroduce the tiger to a special habitat in the country’s south, one of the most ambitious rewilding programs anywhere on Earth. clusters.title: Panama’s golden frogs return to the wild after 17-year battle with deadly fungus
clusters.summary: BY THE OPTIMIST DAILY EDITORIAL TEAM For the first time in nearly two decades, Panama’s forest streams are once again home to flashes of bright yellow. The Panamanian golden frog (Atelopus zeteki) is being reintroduced to the wild 17 years after a fungal epidemic wiped it out in its native habitat. clusters.title: The German City That Defied McDonald’s and Dumped ‘To-Go’ Waste
clusters.summary: On a sunny afternoon, the McDonald’s at the edge of Tübingen buzzes with customers. Teenagers loiter over fries, a family shares burgers, cardboard cups and boxes pile up on plastic trays. Nothing here suggests rebellion.

items.title: The German City That Defied McDonald’s and Dumped ‘To-Go’ Waste
items.description:
On a sunny afternoon, the McDonald’s at the edge of Tübingen buzzes with customers. Teenagers loiter over fries, a family shares burgers, cardboard cups and boxes pile up on plastic trays. Nothing here suggests rebellion. And yet, this fast-food outpost sits at the center of one of Germany’s most consequential...

clusters.title: Stanford Cures Type-1 Diabetes in Mice Without Insulin or Immune Suppression
clusters.summary: In an experiment that exceeded scientists’ expectations, mice had their type-1 diabetes cured through a double-transplant method. Additionally, there was no host rejection of one one of the two types of transplanted cells, and the immune system didn’t attack the other, resulting in a diabetes cure without any side-effects. Obviously caveats must be drawn from […] The post Stanford Cures Type-1 Diabetes in Mice Without Insulin or Immune Suppression appeared first on Good News Network.
